assets.name: lecture_08_figure_01.png
assets.rationale: None of the candidate frames are useful for lecture-note figures. They are all Stanford University title cards with no blackboard mathematics, diagram, or classroom layout, and the instructions explicitly say to reject title cards even when visually clean.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: This program is brought to you by Stanford University.

assets.name: lecture_08_figure_02.png
assets.subtitle_excerpt: But its potential energy can't go anywhere.
assets.caption_hint: But its potential energy can't go anywhere.

assets.name: lecture_08_figure_03.png
assets.rationale: This frame is the clearest and most useful for note writing because it preserves the full qualitative shape of the potential with minimal occlusion. The long left plateau, sharp drop, local minimum, and rising right-hand branch are all visible at once, while the lecturer’s hand sits low enough that it does not cover the essential turning points of the curve. The neighboring frames are similar, but this one is the most balanced and readable.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: then the value of the potential energy
assets.caption_hint: Potential-energy sketch with plateau and minimum
